items.title: SimpleHelp Remote Access Software Vulnerability
items.description: SimpleHelp remote access software contains a critical vulnerability actively exploited by LockBit affiliates to deploy stealer malware, enabling remote code execution on internet-facing servers.

priority.reason: Confirmed exploited (CISA KEV). Known ransomware campaign use. EPSS 94% probability of exploitation within 30 days. CVSS v3 base score 9.8.

w5h.upgrade_action: Apply current vendor patches for SimpleHelp per the advisory (CISA remediation due date: 2026-07-29)
w5h.why_it_matters: This matters because exploitation is confirmed or actively occurring, not theoretical; EPSS puts exploitation probability at 94% within 30 days; critical severity means full compromise is likely if reached.

impact.affected_versions: SimpleHelp (specific versions not provided, assume all vulnerable until vendor clarifies)
impact.how_to_check: Inventory all SimpleHelp installations and compare versions against the advisory. Review logs on matching hosts for unusual authentication or administrative actions.
